stimulus.content: Mr. Johnson writes a letter to his U.S. Senator. In the letter, he asks the Senator to vote for a new clean air law. He says the law would help protect the environment.

stem: How is Mr. Johnson taking part in government at the national level?

choices.text: By voting in a local election
By running for a state office
By writing to a national leader
By paying his state taxes on time

answer_explanation: Writing to a U.S. Senator is a way citizens take part in national government. A Senator serves at the national level in Congress. Voting in local elections and paying state taxes are forms of participation at other levels of government.
